  "approve": true,
  "feedback": "The remediation correctly sets the required 'retry' value in pwquality configuration, addressing the OpenSCAP rule. It is functional and safe, but the script can be simplified and made more robust.",
  "concerns": [
    "The provided snippet is incomplete and truncates part of the grep command.",
    "It assumes the primary config file is always /etc/security/pwquality.conf even if a fragment overrides it.",
    "Leaving backup *.bak files on disk may expose old configuration data."
  ],
  "suggested_improvements": [
    "Combine the removal and addition steps with a single in‑place edit using sed to avoid multiple file writes.",
    "Add a check to ensure the desired retry value does not exceed the policy maximum before writing.",
    "Securely handle or clean up backup files after successful remediation."
  ],
  "security_score": 8,
  "best_practices_followed": true
}
